title: ophrys lutea subsp. corsica
intro: Ophrys lutea subsp. corsica, also known as the Corsican bee orchid, is a subspecies of the bee orchid plant, characterized by its unique appearance and adaptability to the Mediterranean climate. Traditionally, the Corsican bee orchid has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and treating respiratory problems, although further research is needed to confirm these claims. Herbal preparations of the Corsican bee orchid include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are typically consumed orally or used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
